title: Darkly Charred Portuguese Chicken Skewers
description: A crowd-pleasing Portuguese dish featuring sesame seed and miso-marinate, these skewers are low in calories and big on flavor, with a darkly charred exterior and a glassy-jelly interior. Perfect for any occasion, these skewers are sure to impress.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together miso paste, sesame oil, garlic, ginger, paprika, salt, and black pepper to make the marinade.
Add the chicken to the marinade and toss to coat, then cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat, threading the marinated chicken onto skewers.
Brush the grill or grill pan with oil to prevent sticking, then cook the skewers for 8-10 minutes per side, or until darkly charred.
Brush the skewers with lemon juice during the last minute of cooking.
Sprinkle the skewers with sesame seeds and serve immediately.
Garnish with additional sesame seeds and lemon wedges, if desired.
Let the skewers rest for 5 minutes before serving to allow the juices to redistribute.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
